title: The Real Difference Between Your Choices for Selling Digital Products Without PayPal — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: You're a Nigerian, Ghanaian, or Pakistani digital creator trying to sell ebooks, courses, or other digital products, but PayPal has blocked your country. You're not alone. Many creators face similar roadblocks with Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip, too. Their strict KYC requirements, geo-restrictions, and banking limitations leave you with no choice but to look for alternative solutions. This post will help you cut through the noise and make an informed decision about the best option for your situation.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are traditional payment processors, but they come with restrictive terms. PayPal has a lengthy list of blocked countries, and their requirements for merchants are onerous. Stripe has a similar blocking policy, and their fees can be steep. Gumroad has some geographic restrictions, and their payment processing fees range from 3.5% to 8%. Payhip's fees are slightly lower, but they require a Stripe or PayPal account for each sale. Meanwhile, Core Shop Engine is a crypto-native infrastructure that lets you sell digital products without any of these limitations. It offers a full backend with automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts, all without requiring a payment processor account.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

PayPal and Stripe explicitly block countries like Nigeria, Ghana, and Pakistan due to their banking and regulatory relationships. Gumroad and Payhip, while not blocked outright, have similar restrictions and may lock your account without notice. Core Shop Engine, on the other hand, has no such restrictions. It works seamlessly in countries that traditional payment processors block, allowing you to sell digital products to customers from any geographic location.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

While traditional payment processors like PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad may be faster in some cases, they come with steep fees. PayPal's fees range from 2.9% to 4.4%, while Stripe's fees can reach up to 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction. Core Shop Engine, using NOWPayments as its payment processor, charges a flat fee of 1.8% + $0.30, with no additional fees for instant automated delivery, transaction logs, or order management.
Traditional payment processors block countries like Nigeria, Ghana, and Pakistan, but Core Shop Engine works everywhere.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

Choosing crypto-native infrastructure does not mean your buyers need to understand crypto. The Web3 Wallet Connect module generates QR codes for each supported coin, displays live USD-equivalent prices, and guides buyers through the payment flow in plain language. Most buyers complete a crypto payment in under two minutes, even if it is their first time. NOWPayments handles the transaction confirmation and fires the webhook that triggers automated delivery. From the buyer's perspective, they paid and received their product. From your perspective, you received funds in a wallet you control without any platform intermediary between you and your revenue. That is the practical outcome of building on the right infrastructure from the start.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

Can I use Stripe if I am in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan? Unfortunately, no. Stripe blocks these countries due to their banking and regulatory relationships. However, Core Shop Engine provides a viable alternative, allowing you to sell digital products to customers from any country without the need for traditional payment processors.
Core Shop Engine's flat fee of 1.8% + $0.30 per transaction is lower than most traditional payment processors.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you're searching for a PayPal alternative for Nigeria digital sellers or want to know how to sell digital products without a Stripe account, Core Shop Engine is the answer. Its full backend with automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts makes it the perfect solution for creators in restricted countries. Check out the demo at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html to see how it works.
Your Action Checklist

To start selling digital products with Core Shop Engine, follow these steps: Create an account on the Core Shop Engine website and set up your store. Choose a payment method, such as NOWPayments, and connect it to your Core Shop Engine account. Upload your digital products and set their prices. Configure your automated delivery settings and payment verification processes. Promote your store and start selling! With Core Shop Engine, you can focus on creating and selling your digital products without worrying about payment processor restrictions.
With Core Shop Engine, you can automate delivery, payment verification, and order management, freeing up time for more important tasks.
Core Shop Engine — $49.99. Full backend with bot engine, automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts. No payment processor account required. Works everywhere.
